2606.14211.title: Closing the Reflection Gap: A Free Calibration Bonus for Agentic RL
2606.14211.paper_summary: LLMs are increasingly deployed as agents that interact with external environments and observe feedback such as execution results, error messages, and tool outputs. A well-functioning agent should be able to leverage this feedback to accurately assess its own performance. Yet we find a persistent reflection gap: LLM agents tend to mis-assess their own outputs after observing concrete environment feedback -- even for questions they correctly answered -- and standard RL barely helps due to a credit-assignment mismatch. To close this gap, we propose RefGRPO, a simple yet effective fix that augments standard RL algorithms with two key ingredients: a free calibration bonus computed by contrasting the agent's own reflection with the actual outcome (requiring no additional reward model, LLM judge, or external annotation), and a dynamic schedule on its coefficient. Compared to standard RL baselines, our method simultaneously improves reflection calibration (e.g., reduces underconfidence rate $44.4\% \to 7.7\%$) and task accuracy (e.g., $75.1\% \to 76.5\%$) on text-to-SQL across five benchmarks. The resulting calibrated reflection turns the agent into its own verifier grounded in environment feedback, which further enables (i) better self-improvement that uses reflections as pseudo-rewards without outcome supervision, and (ii) more effective test-time selective prediction by committing only to rollouts flagged as correct.
2606.14211.paper_summary_zh: LLMs 正在越來越多地被用作與外部環境互動並觀察反饋（例如執行結果、錯誤信息和工具輸出）的代理。<lb/>一個運作良好的代理應該能夠利用這些反饋準確評估自己的表現。<lb/>然而，我們發現存在一個持續的反思差距：LLM 代理在觀察到具體環境反饋後，往往會錯誤評估自己的輸出——即使是對於他們正確回答的問題——而標準強化學習幾乎無法幫助，因為存在信用分配不匹配。<lb/>為了縮小這個差距，我們提出了 RefGRPO，一種簡單但有效的修正方法，它通過兩個關鍵成分增強標準強化學習算法：一個通過將代理的自我反思與實際結果進行對比計算的免費校準獎勵（不需要額外的獎勵模型、LLM 評判或外部註釋），以及其係數的動態調整計劃。<lb/>與標準強化學習基準相比，我們的方法同時改善了反思校準（例如，將低自信率從 $44.4\% \to 7.7\%$ 降低）和任務準確性（例如，$75.1\% \to 76.5\%$）在五個基準上的文本到 SQL 的表現。<lb/>結果，經過校準的反思使代理成為基於環境反饋的自我驗證者，進一步實現了（i）更好的自我改進，利用反思作為偽獎勵而不需要結果監督，以及（ii）更有效的測試時選擇性預測，僅對標記為正確的回合進行承諾。
2606.14211.author: Yinglun Zhu et.al.
